Home»Sport»Paris Olympics 2024»Paris 2024: Team SA UPDATED medal tally after THOSE Akani Simbine heroics

Silver service.

Bayanda Walaza, Bradley Nkoana, Shaun Maswanganyi and Akani Simbine are all heroes after their scintillating display at the Paris 2024 Olympics. Here’s the updated Team SA medal tally.

Bayanda Walaza, Bradley Nkoana, Shaun Maswanganyi and Akani Simbine have deservedly gotten their hands on silver at the Olympic Games after a sensational performance in the 4x100m relay final. Akani Simbine had a bit of work to do in the final leg after an immensely competitive race, but the 30-year-old got it done and finally laid his hands on some much-deserved silverware on the biggest stage. That must taste sweet for a man who’s made the solo 100m final at three consecutive Olympic Games but narrowly missed out on the podium each time. Less than a week ago, he lost out on a medal by one-hundredth of a second in the biggest race in world sprinting. It was the fastest event of its type in history.

Tatjana Smith became the most decorated Olympian in South African history with her haul oftwo medalsin Paris. She claimed gold in the 100m breaststroke and silver in the 200m event, an exact reversal of her antics in Tokyo. The other Team SA medallists in 2024 areAlan Hatherlywho won bronze in the men’s Mountain Bike (MTB) cross-country race; South Africa’s first Olympic cycling medal since 1956, some 68 years ago, and theRugby Sevens sidewho claimed bronze by beating Australia. The 4x100m relay side takes Team SA’s totalmedal tally to FIVE.A gold, two silvers and two bronzes.

Gardeo Isaacs, Zakhiti Nene, Antonie Nortje and Lythe Pillay finished eighth in Round 1 in 3:03.19 and failed to qualify for the final. However, a post-race disqualification for Nigeria has handed Team SA a lifeline and passage into the4x400m relay final onSaturday 10 August at 21:00. There they’ll face Japan, Zambia, the United States, Belgium, Great Britain, France, Italy andLetsile Tebogo’s Botswana.The newly-crowned 200m Olympic champion is the man of the moment and was a surprise late inclusion in their 4x400m relay team. Immediately they become strong contenders.

View the full schedule via the Paris 2024official website.

title: Paris 2024: Team SA UPDATED medal tally after Simbine heroics
author: James Freemantle
description: Bayanda Walaza, Bradley Nkoana, Shaun Maswanganyi and Akani Simbine are heroes after their scintillating display at the Paris 2024 Olympics.
